docs.title: Welcome to BFC Production Support
docs.text: This is the internal knowledge base for the BFC Production team. It exists to capture how our systems work, how we've solved problems, and how we run repeatable processes — so that knowledge lives in the team, not just in someone's head.

docs.title: What You'll Find Here
docs.text:
Audio — Dante network configuration, console setup, signal flow troubleshooting, and playback systems.

Video — Resolume Arena workflows, codec and file format guides, display and signal troubleshooting.

Lighting — GrandMA3 configuration, Art-Net and sACN setup, fixture patching, and console networking.

Network — VLAN structure, switch configuration, IP addressing, and infrastructure references.

Workflows — Standard operating procedures, repeatable checklists, and step-by-step processes for common tasks.

docs.title: How to Use This Site
docs.text: Use the search bar at the top to find anything quickly. Use the left navigation to browse by category. Pages link to manufacturer documentation where relevant — those links are kept close to the procedures that need them.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: If you've solved a problem, documented a process, or found a better way to do something — add it here. The goal is a knowledge base that gets more useful over time, not one that goes stale. See Creating a New Support Page for instructions on how to add or edit content.

docs.title: Audio
docs.text: This section covers Dante network audio, mixing console setup, signal flow, and playback systems. If you're troubleshooting an audio issue or setting up a new routing configuration, start here.

docs.title: What's in This Section
docs.text:
• Dante — network audio routing, Dante Controller, device patching, and latency settings
  • Consoles — setup, scene management, and show file procedures
  • Signal Flow — how audio moves through the system from source to output
  • Playback — click tracks, backing tracks, and playback system configuration

docs.title: Pages
docs.text: • Loading a Session on the SD12 — Session naming conventions and how to load a session from the Master screen

docs.title: Manufacturer Documentation
docs.text:
• Dante Controller User Guide — Official Audinate reference for Dante Controller

Pages are being added as procedures are documented. If you've solved a problem or established a repeatable process, add it here — see Creating a New Support Page.

docs.title: Loading a Session on the SD12
Loading a Session on the SD12
docs.text: This page covers how to load a session on the DiGiCo SD12 console, including session naming conventions and the steps to load from the Master screen.

docs.title: Session Naming Convention
docs.text:
Sessions are named by date and service order using this format:

YYYY.MM.DD.S
Part Meaning Example YYYY Year 2025 MM Month (zero-padded) 03 DD Day (zero-padded) 16 S Service number 1 or 2

Example: 2025.03.16.1 = March 16, 2025 — first service (9am). 2025.03.16.2 = second service (11am).

docs.title: Loading a Session
docs.text:
Sessions are loaded from the Master screen — the blue touchscreen on the right side of the console.

  1. On the Master screen, tap Sessions
  2. Locate the correct session file by date
  3. Tap the session to select it
  4. Tap Load to load it onto the console

!!! warning "Session Reset Before Service" After loading a session and completing rehearsal, run the Service Reset macro on the SD12 before the walk-in starts. This macro resets critical console settings that are modified during rehearsal. Skipping this step is a common cause of service issues.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The console has two power switches on the back right — both must be on
  • Sessions should be created or confirmed correct during pre-service setup
  • When loading the second service, load the .2 session file — do not reuse the first-service session

docs.title: Lighting
docs.text: This section covers GrandMA3 configuration, Art-Net and sACN setup, fixture patching, and console networking. If you're troubleshooting a lighting issue or integrating the console with other systems, start here.

docs.title: What's in This Section
docs.text:
• GrandMA3 — console setup, show file management, network configuration, and common fixes
  • Art-Net & sACN — protocol setup, universe mapping, and device integration
  • Fixture Patching — adding fixtures, addressing, and profile management
  • Console Networking — VLAN placement, IP configuration, and multi-system integration

docs.title: Pages
docs.text:
• Connecting MA3 to Arena — How to set up Art-Net output from GrandMA3 so Resolume Arena can receive DMX to control the LED wall.

Pages are being added as procedures are documented. If you've solved a problem or established a repeatable process, add it here — see Creating a New Support Page.

docs.title: Connecting MA3 to Arena
docs.text:
It is possible to control the LED wall from the lighting console. In order to do that the MA3 console needs to output Art-Net and Resolume Arena needs to be able to receive Art-Net.

Prior to connecting the MA3 to Arena:

  • The MA3 console needs to be connected to the lighting VLAN (.70)
  • The Arena computer needs to be connected to the lighting VLAN (.70) and the network interface connected to the lighting VLAN needs to be first in the service order.
  • Confirm that the MA3 can control lights
  • Confirm that Arena can control the LED wall on it's own.

!!!!! We have found that it is nessecary to make all network interfaces on the Arena computer inactive in order to get Arena to initially see the Art-Net signal. Once Arena is connected you can go back and make those other network interfaces active. This is necessary to send and receive video via NDI on the Video VLAN (.60) and send and receive audio via DANTE on the Dante VLAN (.51)

docs.title: GrandMA 3 Setup
docs.text: navigate to Settings>Network Protocols > Art Net

docs.title: MA3 settings
docs.text: Enabled: Yes Mode: Broadcast Destination IP: blank Local Universe: 10 Amount: 1 Net: 0 Art-Net Sub-Net: 0 Universe: 10 Art-Net Absolute: 10

docs.title: Arena Setup
docs.text:
• Navigate to arena > preferences > DMX
  • Make sure the correct network adapter is selected.
  • Monitor set to Subnet 0 and Universe 10.

Once you see DMX you can enable the other NIC's again

docs.title: Network
docs.text: This section covers VLAN structure, switch configuration, IP addressing, and infrastructure references. The production network is segmented by discipline — understanding that structure is foundational to troubleshooting any system that touches the network.

docs.title: What's in This Section
docs.text:
• VLAN Structure — how the network is segmented and why (Dante on .51, Video/NDI on .60, Lighting on .70)
  • Switch Configuration — port assignments, trunk configuration, and managed switch access
  • IP Addressing — static assignments, device naming conventions, and address ranges by VLAN
  • Infrastructure References — diagrams, rack documentation, and physical layout

Pages are being added as procedures are documented. If you've solved a problem or established a repeatable process, add it here — see Creating a New Support Page.

docs.title: Video
docs.text: This section covers Resolume Arena workflows, codec and file format guidelines, NDI configuration, and display and signal troubleshooting. If you're dealing with a video system issue or setting up a new configuration, start here.

docs.title: What's in This Section
docs.text:
• Resolume Arena — composition setup, layer management, output mapping, and common fixes
  • Codecs & File Formats — recommended specs for video assets, export settings, and format compatibility
  • NDI — network video routing, source configuration, and troubleshooting
  • Display & Signal — output troubleshooting, resolution mismatches, and display hardware

docs.title: Pages
docs.text:
• Arena Service Bumper — Auto-Start ProPresenter Timer — How Companion automatically starts a ProPresenter countdown timer when the service bumper plays in Arena, and how to set the timer duration before going live.
  • ATEM Output Configuration — Standard Aux output routing for the ATEM Constellation 8K and required MADI 1 audio settings.
  • CG A Dante Virtual Soundcard — Settings and verification steps for Dante Virtual Soundcard on CG A.
  • Applying a ProPresenter Theme on CG B — How to apply the correct BFC Basic theme to all song slides on CG B before service.

Pages are being added as procedures are documented. If you've solved a problem or established a repeatable process, add it here — see Creating a New Support Page.

docs.title: Arena Service Bumper — Auto-Start ProPresenter Timer
Arena Service Bumper — Auto-Start ProPresenter Timer
Overview
docs.text: Trigger Resolume Arena — Service Bumper clip active (Layer 11, Column 2) Action ProPresenter Clock 10 starts automatically Timer Name Arena - Service Bumper Timer System Bitfocus Companion

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
When the service bumper starts playing in Resolume Arena, Bitfocus Companion detects the active clip and automatically starts a countdown timer in ProPresenter on CG A. This means the timer does not need to be started manually — it fires the moment the bumper goes live.

Signal chain:

  1. Operator triggers the service bumper in Resolume Arena (FOH_Arena — Layer 11, Column 2)
  2. Companion detects the clip via the FOH_Arena connection
  3. Companion fires the CG_A_PP7: Start Clock action (Clock Number 10)
  4. ProPresenter on CG A starts the Arena - Service Bumper Timer countdown

docs.title: Before Playing the Bumper — Set the Timer Duration
docs.text:
The timer duration must be set manually in ProPresenter to match the length of the specific bumper video. This will be different for each bumper.

  • [ ] Get the exact duration of the bumper video
  • [ ] In ProPresenter on CG A, open the Clocks panel
  • [ ] Find Arena - Service Bumper Timer (Clock 10)
  • [ ] Set the countdown duration to match the video length
  • [ ] Confirm the timer is ready before going live

If the timer duration is wrong, it will either expire before the bumper ends or continue running after it finishes.

docs.title: Companion Trigger Reference
docs.text:
The trigger is configured in Companion under Triggers → Arena/Service Bumper to CG A Timer.

Field Value Trigger name Arena/Service Bumper to CG A Timer Event On condition becoming true Connection (condition) FOH_Arena Condition type Selected Clip Layer 11 Column 2 Connection (action) CG_A_PP7 Action Start Clock Clock Number 10

To view or edit: open Companion at 10.1.60.135:8888 → Triggers → select Arena/Service Bumper to CG A Timer.

docs.title: Verifying It's Working
docs.text:
• [ ] Confirm the FOH_Arena connection in Companion is green (Connections page)
  • [ ] Confirm the CG_A_PP7 connection in Companion is green
  • [ ] Confirm the trigger is enabled (toggle is on in the Triggers list)
  • [ ] Trigger the service bumper in Arena — the Arena - Service Bumper Timer in ProPresenter should start counting down automatically within a second or two

docs.title: If the Timer Doesn't Start
docs.text:
• Check that FOH_Arena connection is active and not in error state
  • Check that CG_A_PP7 connection is active
  • Confirm the bumper clip is on the correct layer and column (Layer 11, Column 2) — if the Arena composition has been modified, the clip position may have shifted
  • Verify the trigger is enabled in Companion (Triggers page)
  • As a fallback, start the Arena - Service Bumper Timer manually in ProPresenter via the Clocks panel

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• CG A, CG B, and CG C are the three main graphics playback computers — this automation runs specifically through CG A
  • The ProPresenter slides in the Companion-controlled section include labels for Pre-Show, Service Bumper, and Message Bumper — do not move or delete these slides as Companion relies on their position
  • This timer runs independently of the Pre-Show Countdown and other timers in ProPresenter

docs.title: ATEM Output Configuration
docs.text: This page documents the standard Aux output routing for the ATEM Constellation 8K and the required MADI 1 audio settings.

docs.title: Aux Output Routing
docs.text: Output Source Notes Aux 1 Confidence B Confidence display at front of stage Aux 2 CG C CG C graphics computer output Aux 4 BFC Kiosk Campus kiosk feed; set to BFC Kiosk until walk-in Output 13 ME1 Main program output ME2 Outboard ME2 ME3 Stream ME3 Stream output for RESI

docs.title: MADI 1 Audio Setting
docs.text:
The MADI 1 input must be set to unity and unmuted before each service.

  1. Open ATEM Software Control
  2. Go to the Audio tab
  3. Locate the MADI 1 fader
  4. Set the level to +0.00 (unity)
  5. Set the channel to ON (not AFV or OFF)

!!! warning If MADI 1 is muted or not set to unity, audio from the SD12 will not reach the switcher correctly. Check this during the Gameday Checklist before service.

docs.title: Lower Thirds / Keyers
docs.text:
Check that all lower thirds and side thirds render correctly when keyed before service:

  • Verify CG A keyer via Stream Deck
  • Verify CG B keyer via Stream Deck

See the Gameday Checklist for the full verification steps.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Aux routing should be verified as part of the Sunday Gameday Checklist
  • The ATEM Software Control application runs on ATEM Control Tier 3

docs.title: CG A Dante Virtual Soundcard
docs.text: CG A (Computer Graphics A) runs Dante Virtual Soundcard to route audio from full-screen video playback into the Dante network. This page covers the required settings and how to verify it is working correctly.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
When a full-screen video plays on CG A (via ProPresenter or another application), its audio is routed through Dante Virtual Soundcard onto the Dante network rather than through a local audio output. This allows the A1 to receive and control CG A video audio at the FOH console like any other Dante source.

The corresponding fader on the SD12 is the CG A fader.

docs.title: Dante Virtual Soundcard Settings
docs.text:
Open the Dante Virtual Soundcard application on CG A and confirm these settings:

Setting Value Audio Channels 2×2 Dante Latency 10ms Network Interface USB (or the interface in the .51 VLAN range)

The network interface should show an IP address in the 10.1.51.x range (Dante VLAN). If it shows a different IP, select the correct interface.

docs.title: Verifying Audio is Working
docs.text:
To confirm CG A Dante is routing correctly:

  1. Play a video with audio on CG A
  2. At the SD12, check for signal on the CG A fader
  3. Confirm signal is present and routed through the PA Mix Bus and Broadcast Matrix

This is part of the Sunday Gameday Checklist — coordinate with the Video Engineer to play a test video.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Dante Virtual Soundcard must be running and licensed before audio will pass
  • If CG A audio is absent at the console, first check that the DVS application is active and showing the correct network interface
  • The Dante VLAN is 10.1.51.x — see the network documentation for VLAN details

docs.title: Applying a ProPresenter Theme on CG B
Applying a ProPresenter Theme on CG B
docs.text: CG B requires a specific ProPresenter theme applied to all song slides before each service. This page covers how to apply the correct theme.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Song slides on CG B must use the BFC Basic → 1 Line 1 theme. If the wrong theme is applied, song text will not display correctly on the confidence screens.

This step is completed during pre-service setup as part of the Sunday Gameday Checklist.

docs.title: Applying the Theme
docs.text:
Repeat this process for each song in the service playlist on CG B:

  1. Open the song presentation in ProPresenter on CG B
  2. Click the first slide to select it
  3. Press Cmd+A to select all slides
  4. Right-click any selected slide
  5. Navigate to Themes → BFC Basic → 1 Line
  6. Click to apply

The theme will be applied to all slides in the presentation.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The correct theme path is: Themes → BFC Basic → 1 Line
  • Apply the theme to every song in the service — not just one
  • If the theme does not appear, confirm the BFC Basic theme package is installed on CG B
  • Prayer time slides on CG B should also be checked — see the Gameday Checklist

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text: This section contains standard operating procedures, repeatable checklists, and step-by-step processes for common tasks. These pages are designed to be followed in sequence — use them when running a process for the first time, training someone, or making sure nothing gets missed under pressure.

docs.title: Pages
docs.text:
• Sunday Gameday Checklist — Full pre-service checklist organized by role (A1, Video Engineer, Lighting, Stage Crew, Graphics), with notes and links to detailed pages.
  • ControlFlex System Reference — How to use ControlFlex to control house lights, stage curtains, projector power, and PA power.
  • TV Kiosks — BFC HD (Info Beamer) — How to update slides on the TV kiosk system using Info Beamer.
  • Creating a New Support Page — How to add documentation to this site using Obsidian or the GitHub web editor.

If you have a repeatable process that isn't documented here, add it. The goal is that any task with more than a few steps has a page — so the knowledge lives in the system, not just in someone's head.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
ControlFlex is the system used to control house lights, stage curtains, power for the main projectors and displays, and power for the PA. It is accessible from any computer or device on the BFC network.

Access: http://10.1.70.11:8080/master/

The interface has four tabs: General, Video, Power, and Macros.

docs.title: General Tab
docs.text:
Controls lights and curtains.

Main Lighting provides remote access to the same buttons available on the 5 wall panels near the Sanctuary exits. Options include:

  • House On
  • Curtain Open
  • Worship — Curtain Closed
  • Message — Curtain Closed
  • All Lights Off

Stage Curtain controls the motorized center stage curtain (Open / Stop / Close).

Side Curtains controls the left and right side curtains independently (Up / Stop / Down).

Lock Keypads locks all wall keypads in the room. This should be confirmed locked on the Gameday Checklist before service.

docs.title: Video Tab
docs.text:
Controls power for projection and display systems.

  • Main Projectors — The main projectors are lamp-based and the lamps are expensive to replace. To preserve lamp life, turn them on approximately 30 minutes before an event (8:15am on Sundays). Turn off promptly after the event ends.
  • Confidence Projector — Projector at the back of the room.
  • Confidence Display — Display in front of the stage.
  • Multiviewers — Monitors in the main control room.

docs.title: Power Tab
docs.text:
Controls PA and lighting power, as well as a Fire Alarm Beam Detector bypass.

System Power sections:

  • Amplifiers — Main PA amplifier power
  • Worklights — Work lighting in the room
  • Lighting — Stage lighting power
  • Seat Lights — Under-seat lighting

Sound System Power controls power to the main PA system.

!!! warning "Fire Alarm Bypass" The Power tab includes a Fire Alarm Beam Detector bypass. This is used when haze or other effects would otherwise trigger the alarm. Only enable when needed and disable immediately after.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• ControlFlex is on the BFC network — access requires a network connection on-site
  • Projector lamps are the most expensive consumables in the room; never leave them on unnecessarily

docs.title: Creating a New Support Page
docs.text: This guide explains how to add a new documentation page to the BFC Production Support site.

docs.title: Before You Start
docs.text: Every page on this site is a plain Markdown (.md) file stored in the docs/ folder of the GitHub repository. When a change is pushed to GitHub the site rebuilds and publishes automatically within about 60 seconds.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Access to the GitHub repo: BFCProduction/bfc-production-support
  • A GitHub account with access to the repo
  • Basic familiarity with Markdown (see reference at the bottom of this page)

docs.title: Option A — Using Obsidian (recommended for Alan)
docs.text:
• [ ] Open the bfc-production-support/docs vault in Obsidian
  • [ ] Navigate to the correct category folder (audio, video, lighting, network, or workflows)
  • [ ] Create a new file — use lowercase with hyphens, no spaces (example: dante-controller-setup.md)
  • [ ] Write your content using Markdown formatting (see reference below)
  • [ ] If you have images, save them to the images/ folder inside the same category folder
  • [ ] Reference images in your doc like this: ![Description](images/your-image.png)
  • [ ] Push to GitHub — the site will rebuild automatically

docs.title: Option B — Using GitHub Web Editor (recommended for team members)
docs.text:
• [ ] Go to github.com/BFCProduction/bfc-production-support
  • [ ] Navigate to the correct folder under docs/
  • [ ] Click Add file → Create new file
  • [ ] Name the file using lowercase with hyphens, no spaces (example: dante-controller-setup.md)
  • [ ] Write your content in the editor
  • [ ] Click Commit changes with a brief description of what you added
  • [ ] Site rebuilds automatically

docs.title: Adding the Page to the Navigation
docs.text:
New pages do not appear in the site navigation automatically. The mkdocs.yml file in the root of the repo controls the nav and must be updated manually.

  • [ ] Open mkdocs.yml in Obsidian or the GitHub web editor
  • [ ] Find the correct category section under nav:
  • [ ] Add a new line following this format:
  - Audio:
    - Overview: audio/index.md
    - Your New Page Title: audio/your-new-file.md
  • [ ] Save and push — the nav will update on next deploy

docs.title: File Naming Convention
docs.text:
Do Don't dante-controller-setup.md Dante Controller Setup.md resolume-video-stuttering.md Resolume Video Stuttering Fix FINAL.md ma3-artnet-config.md ma3 artnet config v2.md

Lowercase, hyphens only, no spaces, no version numbers in the filename.

docs.title: Category Guide
docs.text: Category Use for audio/ Dante, consoles, signal flow, playback audio video/ Resolume, codecs, display, video signal lighting/ MA3, fixture patching, networking, control network/ Infrastructure, VLANs, switches, IP config workflows/ SOPs, checklists, repeatable processes

docs.title: Markdown Quick Reference
docs.text:
# Heading 1
## Heading 2
### Heading 3

**bold text**
*italic text*

- Bullet item
- Another item

- [ ] Checkbox (unchecked)
- [x] Checkbox (checked)

[Link text](https://example.com)

![Image description](images/your-image.png)

`inline code`

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Never include passwords or credentials in any page — reference the password manager instead
  • If you are documenting a manufacturer system, link to the official docs where possible
  • Keep pages focused on one topic — create a new page rather than making one page too long

docs.title: Sunday Gameday Checklist
docs.text:
The Gameday Checklist is a live Google Sheet filled out each Sunday by the responsible team members. This page explains the structure of the checklist and what each section covers.

Live checklist: Gameday Checklist (Google Sheets)

docs.title: How the Checklist Works
docs.text: Each item has a Responsibility column indicating which role owns it. If you are in that role it is your job to check the box and initial the task as you complete it. All boxes must be checked before the event can start. Use the Comments column to flag anything abnormal — this creates a maintenance record for the team.

docs.title: Service Data
docs.text:
Responsibility: CG

Filled in after rehearsal. Records the actual runtime of each service element so the team can track pacing.

Item Notes 9am Service Runtime HH:MM:SS 9am Message Runtime HH:MM:SS 11am Service Runtime HH:MM:SS 11am Message Runtime HH:MM:SS Flip Time Time between services (MM:SS) Outside Temperature Linked lookup Weather Linked lookup

docs.title: Control Flex
docs.text:
Responsibility: A1, Video Engineer, Lighting

ControlFlex is the system used to control house lights, stage curtains, power for projectors and displays, and power for the PA. See ControlFlex System Reference for full details.

Item Responsibility Amp Power A1 Main Projector Power (Turn on at 8:15am) Video Engineer Confidence Projector Power Video Engineer Confidence Screen Power Video Engineer Multiviewer Power Video Engineer Lighting Power Lighting House Lights are off on the keypad Lighting Keypad locked Lighting Curtain Motors functional A1

docs.title: FOH Audio
docs.text: Responsibility: A1

docs.title: DiGiCo SD12
docs.text:
Item Notes Console is on Two power switches on back right of console The correct session is loaded See Loading a Session on the SD12 Spotify Audio is working Mac Mini on right side of console running Waves Super Rack CG A Audio is working Coordinate with video crew to play a video, confirm signal on CG A fader Sound check for all VIP mics Confirm signal and routing through PA Mix Bus and Broadcast Matrix Line check for worship Ask each band member to play; confirm line and monitor Line check Organ monitor Walk-in snapshot works Fire snapshot and confirm PA responds correctly Bumper snapshot works Fire snapshot and confirm PA responds correctly

!!! warning "After Rehearsal" Before the walk-in starts, hit the Service Reset macro on the SD12. This macro resets a number of critical console settings that are changed during rehearsal. Missing this step is a common cause of service issues.

docs.title: PA
docs.text: Item Notes Main LR is working Confirm PA on in ControlFlex, PA control group unmuted on console Front Fills are working Fed off PA processor — no individual console control; if not working, reset Venueflex Definition Processor Subs are working Surrounds are working Fed from Surround Aux on SD12 via Venueflex Definition Processor; test with Spotify to Surround Aux Spaceflex is working

docs.title: Waves Server
docs.text: Item Notes Waves Server is connected to network Check for green indicator in Waves Superrack Waves is linked to the DiGiCo Waves follows the SD12 automatically when sessions are recalled or cues fired

docs.title: Recorders
docs.text: Item Notes SD Recorder is working Denon SD recorder in FOH rack; put in record-pause mode by pressing record once — auto starts/stops at set times Virtual Soundcheck is working

docs.title: Broadcast
docs.text: Item Notes Broadcast Feed works Confirm Broadcast Wet signal is present at Bcast Wet L+R matrix outputs Lobby speakers on

docs.title: Stage / Backstage
docs.text:
Responsibility: Stage Crew

Item Notes Stage setup and ready for musicians Confirm against stage plot and input list in "00 Prod Doc This Week" folder Wireless mics and monitors have fresh batteries Replace all rechargeable batteries; place removed batteries on charger (top of backstage mic cabinet) Wireless mics in hooks on the appropriate side of the stage Place mics/packs on the side of the stage the person will enter from Stage TV is on and has been tested Confirm video crew is sending signal to it Stage TV cable is neatly stored Cable must unwrap cleanly — moved onto stage by one person during service Podium and/or other stage props are ready to go Normal Sunday: podium, stool, and TV for Pastor Rick Stage east and west manual curtains are in the correct orientation If motorized curtain is open and stained glass is visible, smaller curtains behind must be fully closed Backstage lights / exterior lights are turned off Stage is clear of any unnecessary paper or trash VIPs have mics and know their cues

docs.title: Lighting
docs.text:
Responsibility: Lighting

Item Notes GrandMA is on Power button upper right corner of console; generally left on Correct firmware is loaded Version shown in title bar; current version is 1.9.2.2; restart to auto-load correct version GrandMA is connected to Nodes Feeds 6 ETC Nodes via sACN over lighting VLAN; see DMX Protocols window Correct Arena composition loaded Restart Arena 10 min before service

docs.title: Video
docs.text: Responsibility: Video Engineer / Graphics

docs.title: Switcher (ATEM Constellation 8k)
docs.text: Item Responsibility Notes Video signal from all cameras Video Engineer CCU control is working Video Engineer ATEM Outputs are correct Video Engineer See ATEM Output Configuration Make sure all lower and side thirds look right when keyed Video Engineer Check CG A and CG B keyers via Stream Deck Make sure MADI 1 is set to unity and is unmuted Video Engineer ATEM software → Audio tab → MADI 1 fader at +0.00, set to ON

docs.title: Pro Presenter
docs.text: Item Responsibility Notes All CG computer outputs working Graphics MIDI Connected (CG A, CG B, TD) Graphics CG A Dante running Graphics See CG A Dante Virtual Soundcard Apply correct theme to all songs on CG B Graphics See Applying ProPresenter Theme on CG B Check CG B Prayer time slides Graphics

docs.title: Stream Deck / Companion
docs.text: Item Responsibility Notes Companion is working Video Engineer All sources are connected Video Engineer All buttons are functioning Video Engineer Confirm control is ready for rehearsal Video Engineer Confirm walk-in countdown + preshow adds up to 15 min Video Engineer

docs.title: Recorders (HyperDecks)
docs.text: Item Responsibility Notes All decks have drives Video Engineer 1 drive per HyperDeck, labeled with date and HyperDeck number All drives formatted Video Engineer HyperDeck → Menu → Format → Drive 1 → HFS+ Campus getting kiosk until walk-in Video Engineer Set Aux 4 to BFC Kiosk

docs.title: Stream
docs.text: Responsibility: Video Engineer

docs.title: RESI
docs.text: Item Notes Confirm encoder inputs on Ross Dashboard Open Ross Dashboard on ATEM Control Tier 3; confirm Out 71 [RESI 1] = In 15 [SW Out 15 - ME3]; confirm Out 72 [RESI 2] = In 48 [Hyperdeck 4] Encoder scheduled Web events scheduled for both services Update the date in the "title" on web events Facebook and YouTube feeds scheduled for both services

docs.title: Church Online
docs.text: Item Notes Services scheduled Services have the correct RESI embed link Duration is set to 1 hour 20 minutes Start the video 10 minutes before the service begins

docs.title: Tulakes
docs.text: Item Notes Encoder receiving signal at 10:55 Audio is working

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: System Info Beamer Playlist BFC HD Asset Specs 1920 x 1080 PNG/JPG Dropbox Folder /production/pro presenter/TV Channels/BFC HD

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• [ ] Export slide(s) at exactly 1920 x 1080
  • [ ] Save as PNG or JPG
  • [ ] Name each file: YYYY.MM.DD - Slide Title (example: 2026.02.23 - Student Night)
  • [ ] Save to Dropbox: /production/pro presenter/TV Channels/BFC HD
  • [ ] Confirm content is final (spelling, dates/times, branding)

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
• [ ] Log into info-beamer.com — see credentials in password manager
  • [ ] Assets: drag new image(s) into the upload drop area; confirm they appear in Assets
  • [ ] Playlists: Edit the BFC HD playlist
  • [ ] Bottom of playlist: + Add Assets → select asset(s) → Add to playlist
  • [ ] Reorder slides if needed
  • [ ] Save and Apply Playlist; wait for confirmation

docs.title: Verification
docs.text: • [ ] Check at least one kiosk (or preview) to confirm proper display and readability

docs.title: Quick Fix / Rollback
docs.text:
• [ ] Playlists → BFC HD → Edit; remove new slide(s) or move out of rotation
  • [ ] Save and Apply Playlist; re-check kiosk display
